Fin Whale Modeling Template

# Load in the libraries
library(tidyverse)
## ── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
## ✔ dplyr     1.1.4     ✔ readr     2.1.5
## ✔ forcats   1.0.0     ✔ stringr   1.5.1
## ✔ ggplot2   3.5.2     ✔ tibble    3.2.1
## ✔ lubridate 1.9.4     ✔ tidyr     1.3.1
## ✔ purrr     1.0.4     
## ── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
## ✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
## ✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
## ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors
library(sdmTMB)
library(ggplot2)
#library(INLA)
library(janitor)
## 
## Attaching package: 'janitor'
## 
## The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     chisq.test, fisher.test
library(sp)
library(readr)
library(ggcorrplot)
library(Metrics)
library(rsample)

# Read in the data
#cal_data <- read.csv("~/Documents/Capstone/capstone-scripps/data/Merged_CalCOFI/STD_CalCOFI_final_merged_data.csv")

cal_data <- read.csv("/Users/josh/Documents/GitHub/capstone-scripps/data/Merged_CalCOFI/STD_CalCOFI_final_merged_data.csv")

View(cal_data)
# Plotting Correlation
# Define expected columns
cols_to_use <- c("est_depth", 
                 "temperature_depth_105", "temperature_depth_280", "temperature_depth_55", 
                 "salinity_depth_105", "salinity_depth_280", "salinity_depth_55", 
                 "meridional_velocity_depth_105", "meridional_velocity_depth_280", "meridional_velocity_depth_55", 
                 "zonal_velocity_depth_105", "zonal_velocity_depth_280", "zonal_velocity_depth_55", 
                 "vertical_velocity_depth_105", "vertical_velocity_depth_280", "vertical_velocity_55")

# Keep only existing columns
existing_cols <- intersect(cols_to_use, colnames(cal_data))
cor_data <- cal_data[, existing_cols, drop = FALSE]

# Compute correlation matrix (use complete.obs to skip missing data)
cor_matrix <- cor(cor_data, use = "complete.obs")

# Correlation plot
ggcorrplot(cor_matrix, 
           lab = TRUE, 
           hc.order = TRUE, 
           lab_size =2,
           tl.cex = 5,
           type = "lower", 
           title = "Correlation Plot")

cor_dep_lar <- c("est_depth","euphasia_abundance", "total_abundance","larvae_count")
finding_cols <- intersect(cor_dep_lar, colnames(cal_data))
cor_data_prey<- cal_data[, finding_cols, drop = FALSE]

cor_matrix_prey <- cor(cor_data_prey, use = "complete.obs")

ggcorrplot(cor_matrix_prey, 
           lab = TRUE, 
           lab_size = 2,
           hc.order = TRUE, 
           type = "lower", 
           title = "Correlation Plot",
           tl.cex = 5)

# TWEEDIE
# Create fin whale response column before splitting
cal_data <- cal_data %>%
  mutate(fin_resp = x20hz_scaled + x40hz_scaled)

# Split data into training and testing sets (90% train)
set.seed(123)
split <- initial_split(cal_data, prop = 0.9)
train_data <- training(split)
test_data  <- testing(split)

# Mean imputation for missing numeric values in train and test sets
train_data <- train_data %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ ifelse(is.na(.), mean(., na.rm = TRUE), .)))

test_data <- test_data %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ ifelse(is.na(.), mean(., na.rm = TRUE), .)))

#View(train_data)
# Create mesh using training data
mesh_train <- make_mesh(train_data,
                        xy_cols = c("X", "Y"),
                        cutoff = 5)
plot(mesh_train)

# Fit spatiotemporal model
fit_spatiotemporal <- sdmTMB(
  fin_resp ~ est_depth_std + temperature_depth_105_std + temperature_depth_280_std +
    temperature_depth_55_std + salinity_depth_105_std +
    meridional_velocity_depth_105_std + meridional_velocity_depth_55_std +
    zonal_velocity_depth_105_std + zonal_velocity_depth_55_std +
    vertical_velocity_depth_105_std + vertical_velocity_depth_55_std +
    larvae_count_std, 
  data = train_data, 
  mesh = mesh_train,
  time = "season",
  family = tweedie(link = "log"), 
  spatial = "off", 
  spatiotemporal = "iid"
)

# Extract coefficients and sanity check
tidy(fit_spatiotemporal, conf.int = TRUE)
## # A tibble: 13 × 5
##    term                               estimate std.error conf.low conf.high
##    <chr>                                 <dbl>     <dbl>    <dbl>     <dbl>
##  1 (Intercept)                        2.16        0.0185  2.12       2.19  
##  2 est_depth_std                     -0.125       0.0207 -0.166     -0.0847
##  3 temperature_depth_105_std         -0.117       0.166  -0.443      0.208 
##  4 temperature_depth_280_std          0.127       0.0833 -0.0365     0.290 
##  5 temperature_depth_55_std          -0.0636      0.121  -0.301      0.173 
##  6 salinity_depth_105_std            -0.0539      0.0222 -0.0974    -0.0104
##  7 meridional_velocity_depth_105_std -0.198       0.117  -0.428      0.0319
##  8 meridional_velocity_depth_55_std   0.230       0.116   0.00194    0.458 
##  9 zonal_velocity_depth_105_std      -0.176       0.127  -0.426      0.0735
## 10 zonal_velocity_depth_55_std        0.155       0.128  -0.0952     0.405 
## 11 vertical_velocity_depth_105_std   -0.000932    0.0192 -0.0385     0.0366
## 12 vertical_velocity_depth_55_std    -0.0127      0.0160 -0.0440     0.0186
## 13 larvae_count_std                  -0.0242      0.0243 -0.0718     0.0234
sanity(fit_spatiotemporal)
## ✔ Non-linear minimizer suggests successful convergence
## ✔ Hessian matrix is positive definite
## ✔ No extreme or very small eigenvalues detected
## ✔ No gradients with respect to fixed effects are >= 0.001
## ✔ No fixed-effect standard errors are NA
## ✔ No standard errors look unreasonably large
## ✔ No sigma parameters are < 0.01
## ✔ No sigma parameters are > 100
## ✔ Range parameter doesn't look unreasonably large
# Predictions on training and test data
training_pred <- predict(fit_spatiotemporal, newdata = train_data)
training_pred$observed <- train_data$fin_resp

test_pred <- predict(fit_spatiotemporal, newdata = test_data)
test_pred$observed <- test_data$fin_resp

# Plotting the predictions
p0 <- ggplot(training_pred, aes(x = observed, y = est)) + 
  geom_point() + 
  ggtitle("Train") + 
  geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1, col = "red")

p1 <- ggplot(test_pred, aes(x = observed, y = est)) + 
  geom_point() + 
  ggtitle("Test") + 
  geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1, col = "red")

gridExtra::grid.arrange(p0, p1)

# Compute RMSE
rmse_train <- rmse(training_pred$observed, training_pred$est)
rmse_test <- rmse(test_pred$observed, test_pred$est)

# Compute Range
range_train <- range(train_data$fin_resp, na.rm = TRUE)
range_test <- range(test_data$fin_resp, na.rm = TRUE)

# Compute Standard Deviation
sd_train <- sd(train_data$fin_resp, na.rm = TRUE)
sd_test <- sd(test_data$fin_resp, na.rm = TRUE)

# Assemble the table
summary_table <- data.frame(
  Dataset = c("Training", "Test"),
  RMSE = c(rmse_train, rmse_test),
  Range_Min = c(range_train[1], range_test[1]),
  Range_Max = c(range_train[2], range_test[2]),
  Std_Dev = c(sd_train, sd_test)
)

# Print the summary table
print(summary_table)
##    Dataset     RMSE Range_Min Range_Max  Std_Dev
## 1 Training 21.77891         0  728.0899 20.36466
## 2     Test 15.64797         0  192.6108 14.19314
# DELTA GAMMA
# Create fin whale response column before splitting
cal_data <- cal_data %>%
  mutate(fin_resp = x20hz_scaled + x40hz_scaled)

# Split data into training and testing sets (90% train)
set.seed(123)
split <- initial_split(cal_data, prop = 0.9)
train_data <- training(split)
test_data  <- testing(split)

# Mean imputation for missing numeric values in train and test sets
train_data <- train_data %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ ifelse(is.na(.), mean(., na.rm = TRUE), .)))

test_data <- test_data %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ ifelse(is.na(.), mean(., na.rm = TRUE), .)))

# Create mesh using training data
mesh_train <- make_mesh(train_data,
                        xy_cols = c("X", "Y"),
                        cutoff = 5)
plot(mesh_train)

# Fit spatiotemporal model
fit_spatiotemporal <- sdmTMB(
  fin_resp ~ est_depth_std + temperature_depth_105_std + temperature_depth_280_std +
    temperature_depth_55_std + salinity_depth_105_std +
    meridional_velocity_depth_105_std + meridional_velocity_depth_55_std +
    zonal_velocity_depth_105_std + zonal_velocity_depth_55_std +
    vertical_velocity_depth_105_std + vertical_velocity_depth_55_std +
    larvae_count_std, 
  data = train_data, 
  mesh = mesh_train,
  time = "year",
  family = delta_gamma(link1 = "logit", link2 = "log"), 
  spatial = "off", 
  spatiotemporal = "iid"
)

# Extract coefficients and sanity check
tidy(fit_spatiotemporal, conf.int = TRUE)
## # A tibble: 13 × 5
##    term                              estimate std.error conf.low conf.high
##    <chr>                                <dbl>     <dbl>    <dbl>     <dbl>
##  1 (Intercept)                        2.18       0.237    1.71      2.64  
##  2 est_depth_std                     -0.545      0.108   -0.756    -0.334 
##  3 temperature_depth_105_std         -0.832      0.616   -2.04      0.377 
##  4 temperature_depth_280_std          0.678      0.256    0.175     1.18  
##  5 temperature_depth_55_std           0.117      0.470   -0.804     1.04  
##  6 salinity_depth_105_std            -0.279      0.108   -0.491    -0.0674
##  7 meridional_velocity_depth_105_std -0.542      0.376   -1.28      0.196 
##  8 meridional_velocity_depth_55_std   0.583      0.372   -0.147     1.31  
##  9 zonal_velocity_depth_105_std      -0.692      0.442   -1.56      0.175 
## 10 zonal_velocity_depth_55_std        0.572      0.443   -0.297     1.44  
## 11 vertical_velocity_depth_105_std    0.120      0.0751  -0.0271    0.267 
## 12 vertical_velocity_depth_55_std    -0.00286    0.0616  -0.124     0.118 
## 13 larvae_count_std                   0.0197     0.0776  -0.132     0.172
sanity(fit_spatiotemporal)
## ✔ Non-linear minimizer suggests successful convergence
## ✔ Hessian matrix is positive definite
## ✔ No extreme or very small eigenvalues detected
## ✔ No gradients with respect to fixed effects are >= 0.001
## ✔ No fixed-effect standard errors are NA
## ✔ No standard errors look unreasonably large
## ✔ No sigma parameters are < 0.01
## ✔ No sigma parameters are > 100
## ✔ Range parameters don't look unreasonably large
# Predictions on training and test data
training_pred <- predict(fit_spatiotemporal, newdata = train_data, type = "response")
training_pred$observed <- train_data$fin_resp

test_pred <- predict(fit_spatiotemporal, newdata = test_data, type = "response")
test_pred$observed <- test_data$fin_resp

# Plotting the predictions
p0 <- ggplot(training_pred, aes(x = observed, y = est)) + 
  geom_point() + 
  ggtitle("Train") + 
  geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1, col = "red")

p1 <- ggplot(test_pred, aes(x = observed, y = est)) + 
  geom_point() + 
  ggtitle("Test") + 
  geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1, col = "red")

gridExtra::grid.arrange(p0, p1)

# Compute RMSE
rmse_train <- rmse(training_pred$observed, training_pred$est)
rmse_test <- rmse(test_pred$observed, test_pred$est)

# Compute Range
range_train <- range(train_data$fin_resp, na.rm = TRUE)
range_test <- range(test_data$fin_resp, na.rm = TRUE)

# Compute Standard Deviation
sd_train <- sd(train_data$fin_resp, na.rm = TRUE)
sd_test <- sd(test_data$fin_resp, na.rm = TRUE)

# Assemble the table
summary_table <- data.frame(
  Dataset = c("Training", "Test"),
  RMSE = c(rmse_train, rmse_test),
  Range_Min = c(range_train[1], range_test[1]),
  Range_Max = c(range_train[2], range_test[2]),
  Std_Dev = c(sd_train, sd_test)
)

# Print the summary table
print(summary_table)
##    Dataset     RMSE Range_Min Range_Max  Std_Dev
## 1 Training 18.05597         0  728.0899 20.36466
## 2     Test 14.37996         0  192.6108 14.19314
